cases.name: permissioned-weekly-curation-is-send-ready
cases.prompt: Prepare the promised weekly editorial roundup for subscribers who explicitly opted into product-operations content. Sender, suppression, QA, tracking, and the click-to-guide KPI are verified.

cases.name: interest-segmented-local-edition-is-send-ready
cases.prompt: Send a monthly local-events newsletter only to subscribers who selected that topic and location. The issue has one RSVP CTA, supporting links, consent, exclusions, QA, and registration conversion tracking.

cases.name: controlled-send-time-test-is-send-ready
cases.prompt: Run a consented biweekly newsletter timing test using a recipient segment, one changed variable, click-through winning metric, and all standard pre-send checks.

cases.name: promised-quarterly-digest-is-send-ready
cases.prompt: Send a quarterly research digest to subscribers who chose that topic and cadence. It includes a verified primary CTA, concise mobile sections, UTMs, conversion event, and unsubscribe guardrails.

cases.name: subscriber-preference-edition-is-send-ready
cases.prompt: Send a monthly design newsletter matching subscribers' recorded preferences. It has one primary workshop CTA, verified facts, clear sections, a downstream registration KPI, and completed rendering and seed-send checks.

cases.name: address-without-editorial-consent-is-blocked
cases.prompt: Use a customer email collected for a receipt to send a recurring editorial newsletter. No permission for ongoing marketing is recorded.

cases.name: unpromised-frequency-increase-is-blocked
cases.prompt: Subscribers agreed to a monthly sustainability digest. Marketing wants to switch immediately to daily deal emails with the same list.

cases.name: transactional-stream-near-miss-is-blocked
cases.prompt: Send an otherwise permissioned editorial newsletter through the password-reset transactional stream to avoid the marketing queue.

cases.name: suppressed-subscriber-is-blocked
cases.prompt: Include an unsubscribed recipient in a permissioned newsletter audience because they clicked a recent article.

cases.name: open-only-success-metric-is-held
cases.prompt: Plan a newsletter whose only stated success criterion is maximizing open rate; no click, conversion, reply, revenue, or retention outcome is defined.

cases.name: link-dump-near-miss-is-held
cases.prompt: A draft contains dozens of equal-priority links, no issue-level objective, and no primary CTA. Consent and technical checks otherwise pass.

cases.name: universal-best-time-claim-is-held
cases.prompt: Schedule every newsletter for 10 AM because it is claimed to be the universal best time. No recipient data or timing experiment supports the choice.
